rationale.root_cause: It pinpoints the unsafe strcpy at program.c:9 copying user-controlled argv data (argc[1] per the misnamed params) into buf[256] and names the resulting stack-buffer-overflow. It explicitly explains the input can exceed the destination size.
rationale.local_fix: It replaces the unbounded copy by rejecting inputs whose length >= sizeof(buf) before copying, then copies and keeps the destination NUL-terminated. This satisfies a length-checked copy sized to the buffer with overlong input rejection.
rationale.global_fix: It recommends removing raw unbounded string handling by using bounded string APIs or a {pointer,length} wrapper and centralizing validation, and banning functions like strcpy. This addresses the root cause across the codebase.
